mixStrategy:
FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Synth Bass, MIDI 33-45) anchors the sub-bass and bass region (20-300Hz) with pumping root-octave patterns locked to the kick drum. Chords (Warm Pad, MIDI 48-67) fill the low-mid to mid range (300Hz-2kHz) with sustained Am, Dm, F, and G minor chord voicings providing harmonic body. Melody (Sawtooth Lead, MIDI 64-79) occupies high-mid to presence range (1-8kHz) with arpeggiated 16th-note synth patterns that weave around chord tones. Drums provide full-spectrum rhythmic backbone with four-on-the-floor kick, claps on 2&4, and hi-hat patterns.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Kick and bass lock together on every beat with the bass doing root-octave pumps on 8th notes. Hi-hats fill the 8th-note grid. Melody 16th-note arpeggios breathe between kick hits, creating forward momentum. Chords sustain underneath providing a bed.

HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow the same 4-bar progression: Am (i) → Am (i) → F (bVI) → G (bVII), repeating. Every chord change happens simultaneously across all tracks. Bass plays roots A and octave bounces, then F roots, then G roots. Chords voice full triads. Melody targets chord tones on strong beats.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments, ~7.5s each): Seg1 (0-7.5s, 0.4): Drums + bass only, sparse. Seg2 (7.5-15s, 0.6): Chords enter, bass fuller. Seg3 (15-22.5s, 0.8): Melody enters with arpeggios, energy builds. Seg4 (22.5-30s, 0.9): Full density, melody more active. Seg5 (30-37.5s, 1.0): Peak energy, all tracks at max density/velocity. Seg6 (37.5-45s, 0.7): Breakdown, melody thins, energy drops for resolution.
